commit.message:
feat(timeout): add deadline and timeout utilities (#498)

New package for running functions with time limits:
- Do[T]: run function with timeout, return result or timeout error
- DoSimple: simplified Do for functions returning only error
- After[T]: run function async, receive result on channel
- Race[T]: run multiple functions, return first result

All functions are context-aware and cancel child contexts on completion.
Includes doc.go, 15+ unit tests, 2 benchmarks, 3 examples.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Track supports displaced by playing another support separately so card data can match the official Nasha Town behavior. Add a regression test to keep the replacement-dispose flow from retriggering self-dispose effects.

Scribe: OB-Xd VST3 bridge integration session complete

- Orchestration logs: Spike (architecture), Jet (deployment), Faye (host diagnostics), Ed (verification), Coordinator (integration)
- Session log: Final bridge message-pump refactor validated
- Decision inbox merged: 6 entries covering IPlugFrame fix, versioned deployment, thread affinity root cause
- Cross-agent history updated: Team learnings documented
- Archive check: decisions.md at 152KB, entries all recent (no archiving needed)

Architectural invariant locked:
> The bridge main thread is the UI/STA thread. All window creation, COM operations, plugin loads, and IPlugView calls happen on this thread. Pipe reader is background thread only.

Validation complete:
✅ Shipped Debug bridge byte-matches rebuilt Release bridge
✅ load_ack succeeds; openEditor successfully calls attached() without deadlock
✅ OB-Xd editor incomplete UI confirmed as plugin-side (beyond minimal host scope)
✅ Versioned deployment + manifest working end-to-end
⚠️ Test coverage gap flagged: 0 automated tests currently cover VST3/UI behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix VST3 editor deadlock: move pipe I/O off main thread

Root cause: JUCE-based plugins (OB-Xd) bind their MessageManager to the
thread that loads the plugin DLL (the bridge main thread). The editor
window was created on a separate editorThread_. When attached() called
MessageManager::callFunctionOnMessageThread(), JUCE posted to the main
thread and blocked -- but the main thread was stuck in ReadFile(pipe).

Fix: refactor Bridge::Run() so the main thread runs a Win32 message
loop (GetMessageW). Pipe reading moves to a background thread that
posts WM_BRIDGE_COMMAND to a hidden message-only window. All VST3
plugin loads and editor operations now happen on the main thread, which
is both the COM/STA thread and the JUCE MessageManager thread.

Key changes:
- bridge.h/cpp: main thread runs message loop; PipeReaderLoop on
  background thread; DrainCommandQueue dispatches via window message
- audio_renderer.h/cpp: removed editorThread_, promise/future, timeout;
  OpenEditor is synchronous on calling thread; CloseEditor is direct
  cleanup with no thread join
- EditorHostWindowProc: WM_CLOSE calls CloseEditor for proper VST3
  teardown; uses SetPropW/GetPropW instead of GWLP_USERDATA
- CMakeLists.txt: added commoniids.cpp for IPlugFrame/IPlugView IIDs
- bridge shutdown: ipc_.Close() before join to unblock ReadFile
- OleInitialize moved to Bridge::Run() main thread (was editor thread)

Also includes prior-session uncommitted fixes:
- Version-stamped bridge deployment (csproj)
- IComponentHandler stub in HostApplication
- ParseEditorAck ok-field check + ResolveBridgeExecutablePath

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add `export-audit` CLI command for wallet transaction CSV export (#7700)

Wallet needs to export transactions as a CSV for auditing, with optional
date range filtering and fiat conversion.

## Changes

### `cli.rs`
- New `ExportAuditArgs` struct with: `--output-file`, `--start-date`,
`--end-date`, `--conversion-rate`, `--currency` (default: `USD`)
- New `ExportAudit(ExportAuditArgs)` variant in `CliCommands`
- `parse_utc_datetime` helper accepting RFC3339 and `YYYY-MM-DD` /
`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` formats

### `commands.rs`
- `ExportAudit` handler: fetches all completed transactions, filters by
date range, calls `write_audit_to_csv_file`
- `write_audit_to_csv_file`: emits CSV with columns matching the spec —
transaction type is `Deposit` (inbound/coinbase) or `Withdraw`
(outbound); transaction hash derived from first kernel hash; amounts in
XTM (Minotari); optional fiat column using supplied rate

### `wallet_modes.rs`
- Added `ExportAudit` arm to the exhaustive command-match test

## Example

```bash
minotari_console_wallet export-audit \
  --output-file audit.csv \
  --start-date 2021-01-01 \
  --end-date 2021-12-31 \
  --conversion-rate 3130.0 \
  --currency USD
```

Output CSV columns:
```
ID, Transaction Hash, Status, Transaction Type, DateTime (UTC),
From Address, To Address, Amount, AmountTicker, Amount (USD),
Txn Fee, FeeTicker, Fee (USD)
```

commit.message:
feat(web-ui): add Edit Task dialog to task detail page

Add EditTaskDialog component that allows operators to edit task metadata
from the task detail page. The dialog pre-populates all editable fields
from the current task data and uses optimistic concurrency control via
the version field.

Features:
- Edit button with pencil icon in task detail header
- Pre-populated form for all mutable fields (title, description,
  priority, risk level, estimated size, external ref, severity,
  acceptance criteria, definition of done, required capabilities,
  suggested file scope)
- Only changed fields sent in update payload
- 409 Conflict handling with user-friendly message
- 15 component tests + 2 integration tests in TaskDetailPage

Closes T129

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(control-plane): clean orphaned worktrees on startup (T149)

Add WorkspaceCleanupService that runs during application bootstrap to
scan workspace directories, cross-reference with active leases in the
database, and auto-clean orphaned worktrees exceeding the retention
period (default 7 days, configurable via WORKSPACE_RETENTION_DAYS).

- Scans {WORKSPACES_ROOT}/{repoId}/{taskId}/ directory structure
- Never deletes worktrees with non-terminal leases (safety invariant)
- Orphans older than retention → deleted with size estimation
- Recent orphans → logged as pending cleanup
- Error isolation: one directory failure doesn't prevent others
- Cleans empty repo directories after workspace deletion
- 17 unit tests with fake filesystem for deterministic testing
- Registered WorkspaceCleanupModule in AppModule after StartupDiagnostics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(web-ui): add Create Worker Pool dialog to Pools page

Implement T127 — Create Worker Pool dialog component with form fields
for name (required), poolType (required, select), provider, model,
maxConcurrency (default 3), and defaultTimeoutSec (default 3600).

- Create CreatePoolDialog component using shadcn Dialog + Select
- Add 'Create Pool' button to PoolsPage header
- Wire to existing useCreatePool hook with cache invalidation
- 11 comprehensive tests covering rendering, validation, submission,
  error handling, form reset, and whitespace rejection
- Add jsdom polyfills for Radix UI Select in test environment

localCheckoutStrategy (select: worktree/clone, default 'worktree').

on success. Add 'Add Repository' button to dashboard, visible when
a project is selected via ProjectSelector.

18 comprehensive tests covering rendering, defaults, validation,
submission, error handling, and cancel behavior.

- Step 1: Path input with optional glob pattern and scan button
- Step 2: Preview table with checkboxes, parse warnings, editable names
- Step 3: Confirmation summary before committing the import
- Step 4: Result display with created/skipped/error counts

Add "Import Tasks" button to the Tasks page header. Includes 22 tests
covering all step transitions, API payloads, error handling, selection
toggling, and state reset on close/reopen.

Closes T118.

hook. Place the 'Create Project' button on the Dashboard page for initial

Closes T125.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(control-plane): add startup recovery diagnostics logging (T148)

Add StartupDiagnosticsService that runs on application bootstrap to
detect and log pending recovery items from unclean shutdowns:

- Stale leases: heartbeat > 75s old (matching reconciliation sweep policy)
- Orphaned jobs: CLAIMED/RUNNING status > 10 min old
- Stuck tasks: ASSIGNED status > 5 min old

Clean startup logs reassuring INFO message; recovery items log WARN
with counts. Errors are caught so diagnostics never block startup.

15 unit tests cover all detection scenarios and error handling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Extract insertProject() and insertRepository() helper functions

Automates submitting prediction engine picks to iTipFooty:

- Map prediction engine picks (team IDs) to iTipFooty H/A selections

- Handle locked games (already kicked off) gracefully

Backend:
- New service: backend/src/services/itipfooty.ts
- New route: /api/itipfooty/status and /api/itipfooty/submit
- Credentials loaded from .env (ITIPFOOTY_USERNAME, PASSWORD, COMP_ID)
- dotenv added for env var loading
- Submission logged to DataSourceLog

Frontend:
- Settings page: iTipFooty Integration card with status display
- Submit Tips button with detailed result feedback per game
- Shows configuration instructions when not configured

Security:
- Credentials stored in backend/.env (gitignored)
- .env.example provided with empty placeholders
- Status endpoint masks username with ***

Add the `factory init` subcommand that registers a project with the
factory through an interactive flow:

- Prompts for missing values via readline
- Creates Project and Repository records in SQLite
- Optionally discovers and imports tasks from a local directory

- Handles Ctrl+C gracefully without partial writes

Restructures cli.ts to support Commander subcommands while preserving
the default server-start behavior.

Closes T143.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix three existing bugs in bioc-run (#23)

* Fix three existing bugs in bioc-run

1. Fix path case inconsistency: Use uppercase R in all volume mount paths
   - Changed /usr/local/lib/r/host-site-library to /usr/local/lib/R/host-site-library
   - Affects reset ownership (-r) and permission-fixing operations
   - Critical on case-sensitive filesystems (Linux)

2. Fix variable name typo: Use HLIBUSER instead of undefined HLIBENV
   - Line 142 was checking wrong variable name
   - Caused R_LIBS_USER to always be appended to .Renviron

3. Fix regex pattern: Remove pipe from character class
   - Changed GITHUB_[P|T].* to GITHUB_[PT].*
   - Inside brackets, pipe is literal, not alternation

feat(cli): implement two-phase Ctrl+C shutdown

Add graceful drain on first SIGINT/SIGTERM that polls SQLite for active
worker leases (up to 30s), then flushes tracing and closes the app.
Second SIGINT force-kills tracked child processes and exits immediately.

- Create apps/cli/src/shutdown.ts with countActiveLeases, drain,
  forceKillChildren, setupShutdownHandlers, and childPids export
- Replace inline signal handlers in cli.ts with setupShutdownHandlers()
- Add 22 unit tests covering all exported functions
- Mark T147 as done in backlog

fix: Use AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY env var instead of hardcoded claude (#3100)

* fix: use AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY env var instead of hardcoded claude

The recipe runner and delegation scripts hardcoded 'claude' as the agent
binary, causing failures when amplihack is run with other agents (copilot,
codex, etc.).

Changes:
- cli.py: Set AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY env var at dispatch for all 5 agent cmds
- rust_runner.py: Forward --agent-binary flag to Rust recipe runner
- platform_cli.py: Read env var instead of hardcoding 'claude'
- delegate_response.py (3 mirrors): Read env var + add missing 'import os'

The Rust recipe runner companion changes are in
rysweet/amplihack-recipe-runner#29.

Fixes #3099

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Fix orchestrator.py: use ${AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY:-claude} bash fallback
- Fix executor.py (2 locations): same bash fallback pattern for remote execution
- Fix empty string edge case: use 'or "claude"' pattern instead of default param
  in platform_cli.py and all 3 delegate_response.py mirrors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(cli): implement project metadata auto-detection for factory init

Add detect.ts module with five detection functions used by `factory init`
to minimize interactive prompts:

- detectProjectName: reads package.json name, falls back to dir basename
- detectGitRemoteUrl: runs `git remote get-url origin`
- detectDefaultBranch: parses git symbolic-ref, defaults to "main"
- detectOwner: reads git config user.name, falls back to OS username
- detectAll: aggregates all detections into ProjectMetadata

All functions return null on failure (never throw). 21 tests using real
temp directories and git repos validate all detection paths and fallbacks.

Closes T142.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
